skill.name: creating-claude-rules
skill.description: Guides users who create, fix, or migrate Claude modular rule files in .claude/rules/.
skill.summary: Create valid Claude rule files with correct paths frontmatter and glob pattern usage.
skill.author: AgentWorkforce

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as external command execution and a documentation link as network behavior. Manual review found only instructional markdown for Claude rule files, with no executable scripts, command invocation, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Examples
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings are markdown inline code, fenced YAML examples, directory examples, and glob pattern examples. They do not execute shell, Ruby, or other external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose, tables, and fenced markdown examples. No executable source file or command runner is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is a plain reference link to Claude documentation. The skill contains no code that sends network requests or transmits local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 121 is a documentation reference in markdown. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, or other network execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography detections do not correspond to cryptographic code. Manual review found no hashing, encryption, signing, or credential handling logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain frontmatter and a comparison table about Claude and Cursor rule fields. No cryptographic algorithm or implementation appears there.

content.value_statement: Claude rule files can fail when teams reuse Cursor fields or invalid glob syntax. This skill gives clear patterns for valid .claude/rules/ files, scoped paths, and migration cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Explains how .claude/rules/ files are organized and loaded.
Shows valid paths frontmatter for file-scoped Claude rules.
Distinguishes Claude paths from Cursor globs frontmatter.
Explains when to omit paths for global rules.
Provides examples of quoted glob patterns for common file scopes.
Helps migrate Cursor-style alwaysApply rules to Claude format.
limitations: Does not validate rules by running Claude Code.
Does not generate project-specific conventions without user input.
Does not cover every undocumented Claude behavior.
Does not provide automation scripts for rule conversion.

use_cases.title: Set Up Project Rules
use_cases.description: Create clear rule files for coding standards, testing conventions, or team workflows.

use_cases.title: Fix Broken Rule Frontmatter
use_cases.description: Replace Cursor-style fields with Claude-compatible paths and global rule structure.

use_cases.title: Migrate Cursor Rules
use_cases.description: Translate existing rule files into a Claude rules directory with correct scope behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Rule
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Claude rule for our TypeScript import style. It should apply to all TypeScript files under src and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Existing Frontmatter
prompt_templates.scenario: Correct a rule copied from Cursor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Claude rule frontmatter and rewrite it using valid .claude/rules/ syntax. Explain what changed and why.

prompt_templates.title: Design Rule Organization
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan several rule files for a repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest a .claude/rules/ directory structure for frontend, backend, tests, and documentation rules. Include the scope for each file.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate a Rule Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these Cursor rule definitions into Claude modular rules. Preserve intent, remove unsupported fields, and identify any global rules.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a rule that applies to all TypeScript and TSX files.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a path-scoped Claude rule with quoted patterns for TypeScript and TSX files, plus concise rule content.

output_examples.input: A user provides a rule with globs and alwaysApply fields.
output_examples.output: The response explains that globs and alwaysApply are Cursor-specific, then rewrites the rule using Claude paths or no frontmatter.

output_examples.input: A user wants separate standards for frontend, tests, and markdown.
output_examples.output: The response proposes organized rule files, each with a clear scope and short guidance for the relevant files.

best_practices: Quote glob patterns in paths frontmatter to avoid YAML parsing errors.
Use no frontmatter for rules that should apply to every file.
Keep each rule focused on one topic or project area.
anti_patterns: Do not use Cursor-only fields such as globs or alwaysApply.
Do not place unrelated guidance in one large rule file.
Do not use unquoted glob patterns in YAML frontmatter.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Claude modular rule files under .claude/rules/ with correct scope and frontmatter.

faq.question: Can it migrate Cursor rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to replace Cursor globs and alwaysApply fields with Claude-compatible structure.

faq.question: When should paths be omitted?
faq.answer: Omit paths when a rule should apply globally across the project.

faq.question: Why should glob patterns be quoted?
faq.answer: Quoted patterns avoid YAML parsing problems with special characters such as asterisks and braces.

faq.question: Does it run validation commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and examples, but it does not execute validation tools.

faq.question: Is this useful with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. Codex can use the guidance to create or review Claude Code rule files in a repository.
